annotations.label: Quadratic formula

anchor.snippet: is a closed-form expression describing the solutions of a quadratic equation

annotations.note: quadratic_eq_zero_iff states roots equal (-b ± s)/(2a) with s a square root of the discriminant, generalizing the closed-form formula to fields with NeZero (2).

annotations.label: Roots given by the quadratic formula

anchor.snippet: can be found using the quadratic formula

annotations.note: quadratic_eq_zero_iff gives the iff between the quadratic vanishing and x equaling the two formula roots; exists_quadratic_eq_zero gives existence.

annotations.label: Discriminant

anchor.snippet: is known as the discriminant of the quadratic equation

annotations.note: discrim a b c is defined as b ^ 2 - 4 * a * c, exactly the discriminant.

annotations.label: Discriminant determines nature of roots

anchor.snippet: the equation has two distinct real roots

annotations.note: discrim_eq_zero_iff captures the repeated-root case and quadratic_ne_zero_of_discrim_ne_sq/discrim_lt_zero cover non-real cases, but the full real-root trichotomy is not a single statement.

annotations.label: Roots as x-intercepts of the parabola
annotations.note: Mathlib has no geometric notion of a parabola's x-intercepts tied to the quadratic's roots.

annotations.label: Identifying the axis of symmetry

anchor.snippet: can also be used to identify the parabola's axis of symmetry

annotations.note: No notion of a parabola's axis of symmetry exists in Mathlib (grep found no matches).

annotations.label: Equivalent formulation of the quadratic formula

anchor.snippet: The quadratic formula can equivalently be written using various alternative expressions

annotations.note: Mathlib does not provide the alternative algebraic rewritings of the quadratic formula.

annotations.label: Fagnano's formula (root in denominator)

anchor.snippet: describes the same roots via an equation with the square root in the denominator

annotations.note: Fagnano's denominator form of the roots is absent from Mathlib (no matches found).

annotations.label: Catastrophic cancellation

anchor.snippet: Catastrophic cancellation occurs when two numbers which are approximately equal are subtracted

annotations.note: Floating-point catastrophic cancellation is not formalized in Mathlib (grep found no matches).

annotations.label: Pocket-calculator precision-loss example
annotations.note: This numerical precision-loss example has no counterpart in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Brahmagupta's solution in modern notation

anchor.snippet: In modern notation, this can be written

annotations.note: This historical example, though equivalent to quadratic_eq_zero_iff, is not stated as such in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Axis-aligned parabola

anchor.snippet: an axis-aligned parabola is a curve whose

annotations.note: Mathlib has no definition of an axis-aligned parabola (no parabola matches found).

annotations.label: Quadratic formula gives x-axis crossing points

anchor.snippet: the quadratic formula defines the points

annotations.note: The algebraic content (roots are (-b ± s)/(2a)) is quadratic_eq_zero_iff, but the geometric x-axis-crossing interpretation is not formalized.

annotations.label: First term gives axis of symmetry

anchor.snippet: The first term describes the axis of symmetry

annotations.note: The claim that -b/(2a) is the parabola's axis of symmetry has no geometric formalization in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Vertex on x-axis gives repeated root

anchor.snippet: then the corresponding equation has a single repeated root on the line of symmetry

annotations.note: quadratic_eq_zero_iff_of_discrim_eq_zero and discrim_eq_zero_iff formalize the unique repeated root -b/(2a) when the discriminant is zero, but without the geometric vertex framing.

annotations.label: Sign of discriminant and real roots

anchor.snippet: If the discriminant is positive, then the vertex is not on the

annotations.note: discrim_lt_zero and quadratic_ne_zero_of_discrim_ne_sq relate discriminant sign to (non)existence of real roots, but the positive-discriminant geometric statement is not directly formalized.

annotations.label: Dimensional consistency of the coefficients

anchor.snippet: must have the same units, because the terms

annotations.note: Dimensional analysis of the coefficients' units is not formalized in Mathlib.
